reasoning: Since no search tools are available, this relies entirely on the base rate of stock price movements. Over short intervals (1 week to 3 months), stock prices roughly follow a random walk with a very slight positive upward drift, meaning the probability of an increase is typically just above 50% (around 51-53%). PEG typically has an ex-dividend date in early December (often December 8). An ex-dividend date involves a mechanical decrease in the stock price equal to the dividend amount. Because of this, the final resolution date (2026-01-24) faces a slight headwind relative to the cutoff date, mitigating its time-based upward drift. Following the instruction that probabilities for distant dates should be closer to 0.5 due to increasing uncertainty, the estimates decrease slightly toward 0.5 for the later dates.
